theme_strategy.description: A trustworthy pet services marketplace that balances professional reliability with the joy of pet ownership. The vibrant red (#E6202E) signals energy and action, while the creamy off-white background (#F8F8F8) provides a clean, calming canvas.

decorations.waves: Use SVG wave dividers between sections in text-red-500/10

buttons.primary: bg-[#E6202E] hover:bg-[#D31522] text-white font-bold py-3 px-8 rounded-lg shadow-md hover:shadow-lg transition-all transform hover:-translate-y-0.5 active:translate-y-0
buttons.secondary: bg-white border-2 border-[#E6202E] text-[#E6202E] font-bold py-3 px-8 rounded-lg hover:bg-red-50 transition-colors

cards.service_card: bg-white rounded-2xl shadow-sm border border-gray-100 hover:shadow-xl hover:border-red-100 transition-all duration-300 group overflow-hidden
cards.profile_card: bg-white rounded-2xl p-6 shadow-sm border border-gray-100 flex flex-col items-center text-center
cards.pricing_card: relative bg-white rounded-2xl p-8 shadow-md border-2 border-transparent hover:border-[#E6202E] transition-all

inputs.search_bar: h-14 pl-12 pr-4 rounded-full border-gray-200 focus:border-[#E6202E] focus:ring-[#E6202E]/20 shadow-sm text-lg

hero_section.alt: Happy golden retriever on stone surface, symbolizing joy and trust

instructions_to_main_agent: IMPERATIVE: Use the primary color #E6202E for all main calls-to-action (buttons, active states).
IMPERATIVE: Use Montserrat font for all Headings (H1-H6) in uppercase where appropriate for modern look.
IMPERATIVE: Use Lato (or system sans-serif if Lato unavailable) for body text.
NAVIGATION: Create a sticky header with a clean white background and shadow-sm.
HERO SECTION: Use a split layout or a centered layout with a large, high-quality image of a pet (use provided hero assets). Include a prominent search bar.
SUBSCRIPTION CARDS: Design the subscription plan page with 3 distinct cards. Highlight the '12 Months' plan as 'Best Value'. Use checkmarks in primary red.
CONTACT BLOCKING: Implement a visual blur or 'lock' icon overlay on contact details for non-subscribed users.
TRUST: Add a 'Verified Professionals' badge system using the CheckBadge icon from Lucide/Heroicons.
MAPS: Use a placeholder static map image or integration if live map is not ready, but style it to match the clean theme.
RESPONSIVENESS: Ensure all grids collapse to single column on mobile (grid-cols-1) and use generous padding (p-4 mobile, p-8 desktop).

accessibility_rules.contrast: Ensure white text on red background (#E6202E) meets AA standards. Avoid light gray text on white.
accessibility_rules.interactive: All buttons must have :focus-visible styles (ring-2 ring-offset-2 ring-red-500).

universal_guidelines: Dark colors look good when used independently without gradients. DO NOT use dark colors as gradients
Don't make generic centered layouts, simplistic gradients, and uniform styling.
Create depth through layered design elements with z-index hierarchy
Use glass-morphism effects with backdrop filters (12-24px blur) where appropriate (e.g., sticky headers)
You MUST NOT apply universal transition. Eg: transition: all. This results in breaking transforms. Always add transitions for specific interactive elements like button, input excluding transforms
You MUST NOT center align the app container, ie do not add .App { text-align: center; } in the css file. This disrupts the human natural reading flow of text
Always use modern button styles like pill-shaped, or sharp button with interaction animations & color relevant to the app style
NEVER: use AI assistant Emoji characters for icons.
Every interaction needs micro-animations - hover states, transitions, parallax effects, and entrance animations. Static = dead.
Use 2-3x more spacing than feels comfortable. Cramped designs look cheap.
Subtle grain textures, noise overlays, custom cursors, selection states, and loading animations: separates good from extraordinary.
Main agent relies on these image URLs. Missing categories = main agent will use placeholder text which is FORBIDDEN
Prioritize using pre-existing components from src/components/ui when applicable
Create new components that match the style and conventions of existing components when needed
Examine existing components to understand the project's component patterns before creating new ones
Components MUST use named exports (export const ComponentName = ...)
Pages MUST use default exports (export default function PageName() {...})
